events.title: Heatwaves, floods and wildfires pose rising threat to democracy, report finds
events.description: Research shows natural hazards linked to climate crisis disrupted 23 elections in 18 countries in 2024 Democracy is under mounting threat from the climate crisis, with new analysis documenting how elections are increasingly shaped not only by political forces but also by floods, wildfires and extreme weather. At least 94 elections and referendums across 52 countries have been disrupted by climate-related impacts over the last two decades, researchers found. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Heatwaves, floods and wildfires pose rising threat to democracy, report finds

events.title: Two-thirds of global hunger concentrated in 10 conflict-hit countries
events.description: A growing share of global hunger is becoming entrenched in a small group of conflict-hit countries, with two-thirds of people facing acute food insecurity concentrated in just 10 nations, a major international report backed by UN agencies warns.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Two-thirds of global hunger concentrated in 10 conflict-hit countries

events.title: Syrian authorities arrest ex-intelligence officer tied to a leaked video of a massacre
events.description: Syrian authorities have arrested a former intelligence officer allegedly linked to a video showing the fatal shooting of dozens during the country's conflict
events.ticker_label: 💥 Syrian authorities arrest ex-intelligence officer tied to a leaked video of a massacre

events.title: Hamas influence looms over Gaza elections as experts warn vote could backfire
events.description: Hamas reportedly increased its hold in Gaza as Deir al-Balah holds its first elections in 20 years, raising concerns about terrorist group influence.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Hamas influence looms over Gaza elections as experts warn vote could backfire

events.title: Tornado barrels through Oklahoma
events.description: A tornado tore through the US state of Oklahoma on Thursday, ripping roofs off homes and damaging power infrastructure as it struck parts of Enid, a town of about 50,000 near the northern border, with no fatalities reported but residents trapped in their homes and extensive damage recorded, according to local police, in a report by France 2 and Rachael Griffiths.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Tornado barrels through Oklahoma

events.title: Rising nuclear risks put global security pact to the test at UN Headquarters
events.description: At a moment of mounting nuclear danger and deepening global mistrust, governments from nearly every country will convene at the United Nations next week to confront a central question: will the world’s main barrier against the spread of nuclear weapons still hold?

events.title: World News in Brief: Airliner safety warning, drought in Somalia, solar boosts farming in Ethiopia
events.description: The head of the UN’s civil aviation agency, ICAO, has called for immediate global action to protect commercial flights from escalating military threats, including missiles, drones, and GPS signal jamming.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 World News in Brief: Airliner safety warning, drought in Somalia, solar boosts farming in Ethiopia

events.title: US military kills two suspected narco-terrorists in strike on drug-trafficking vessel in the Pacific
events.description: U.S. Southern Command says a lethal strike on a suspected narco-trafficking vessel in the Eastern Pacific killed two suspected narco-terrorists.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 US military kills two suspected narco-terrorists in strike on drug-trafficking vessel in the Pacific

events.title: US military kills two more people in strike on alleged drug boat in Pacific
events.description: Small boat destroyed in video posted on social media as US campaign has killed at least 178 people since September The US military announced on Friday that it killed two people in an attack on a boat in the eastern Pacific, part of a series of deadly strikes on vessels in recent months which it claims are targeting “narco-trafficking” operations. The US Southern Command declared in a social media post on X that Gen Francis L Donovan directed Joint Task Force Southern Spear, the counter-narcotics unit that operates in the region, to carry out a lethal strike. The US military posted a video, which it labeled unclassified, showing a small boat being destroyed in an explosion. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 US military kills two more people in strike on alleged drug boat in Pacific

events.title: Tornado sweeps through Oklahoma City
events.description: On Thursday, a tornado of rare intensity swept through the city of #Enid, Oklahoma, leaving behind a scene of devastation. The disaster left at least 10 people injured. Footage of the event has gone viral on social media.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Tornado sweeps through Oklahoma City

events.title: Middle East war: After oil and gas, concerns grow over minerals crunch
events.description: The shipping crisis in the Strait of Hormuz caused by war in the Middle East has exposed a new threat: a looming shortage of strategic minerals that drive economies all over the world – and a race by countries to obtain them.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Middle East war: After oil and gas, concerns grow over minerals crunch

events.title: Philippines’ ex-president Rodrigo Duterte to face trial for crimes against humanity
events.description: ICC judges say there are substantial grounds to believe Duterte guided anti-drugs crackdown that killed thousands The former president of the Philippines, Rodrigo Duterte , will face trial at the international criminal court (ICC) after judges unanimously confirmed charges of crimes against humanity over his “war on drugs”. Pre-trial judges concluded on Thursday that there were substantial grounds to believe Duterte was responsible for the crimes against humanity of murder and attempted murder in relation to anti-drugs crackdowns that led to the killing of thousands of people. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Philippines’ ex-president Rodrigo Duterte to face trial for crimes against humanity

events.title: World News in Brief: Civilians at risk in Gaza and West Bank, Duterte to go on trial in ICC, Yemen detainees update
events.description: Continued strikes, shelling and gunfire in residential areas of Gaza are raising alarm over civilian safety, the UN relief coordination office OCHA has warned.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 World News in Brief: Civilians at risk in Gaza and West Bank, Duterte to go on trial in ICC, Yemen detainees update

events.title: SECURITY COUNCIL LIVE: Regional war threatens Syria’s fragile transition
events.description: The Security Council is meeting now at a critical juncture for Syria’s fragile transition. While the Iran-US ceasefire has temporarily eased regional spillover from Lebanon and elsewhere, conditions remain volatile in Syria. The reported US military withdrawal now completed, marks a milestone, yet the chamber faces a country grappling with deep internal fissures and external pressures. Follow full meetings coverage below and UN News app users should click here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 SECURITY COUNCIL LIVE: Regional war threatens Syria’s fragile transition

events.title: How frustration at Cop stalemates inspires first global talks on phasing out fossil fuels
events.description: ‘Coalition of the willing’ gathers in Colombia to try to bypass petrostate blockages of Cop summits and chart fresh path The world’s first Transitioning Away from Fossil Fuels conference, co-hosted by Colombia and the Netherlands, takes place in Santa Marta, Colombia, from 24 to 29 April. A “coalition of the willing” – including 54 countries and various subnational governments, civil society groups and academics – will try to chart a new path to powering the world with low-carbon energy. Continue reading...

events.title: Iran: Neither war nor Peace, Ukraine’s Lifeline, Starmer: “Just a Scratch”?
events.description: In a week where a stalemate appears to have developed after nearly two months of asymmetric conflict between the US and Iran, President Trump’s Truth Social posts have swung between belligerent maximalism and breezy deal-making—one minute suggesting a within-reach deal, and that Iranian forces would personally help their enemy confiscate what he called its “nuclear dust” and move it to America.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran: Neither war nor Peace, Ukraine’s Lifeline, Starmer: “Just a Scratch”?

events.title: 40 years since the Chernobyl disaster
events.description: This Sunday marks 40 years since the Chernobyl disaster, which the UN says affected more than 3.5 million people and contaminated nearly 50,000 square kilometres of land, and which remains the worst nuclear accident in history, as France Télévisions gains rare access to the site for a closer look.

events.title: Despite ceasefire, Hormuz tensions continue to throttle supply chains worldwide
events.description: A temporary ceasefire between the United States and Iran has been extended, offering an opening for diplomacy – but persistent tensions in the Strait of Hormuz are compounding trade disruptions and intensifying pressure on humanitarian operations and vulnerable communities far beyond the Gulf.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Despite ceasefire, Hormuz tensions continue to throttle supply chains worldwide

events.title: SECURITY COUNCIL LIVE: Update on Haiti as humanitarian crisis continues alongside gang violence
events.description: The Security Council is addressing the deepening crisis in Haiti on Thursday, centered on the Secretary-General’s latest report which highlights a security landscape of both intensified enforcement and rising civilian risk. While operations including by the UN-backed Gang Suppression Force (GSF) between December and February resulted in the deaths of 1,343 suspected gang members, the humanitarian cost remains staggering. The UN Special Representative told ambassadors elections are the “only legitimate path” back to political stability, while his counterpart with the GSF said its success depends on “effective coordination”. Follow full meetings coverage below.
events.ticker_label: 💥 SECURITY COUNCIL LIVE: Update on Haiti as humanitarian crisis continues alongside gang violence

events.title: Thousands at risk after multi-million dollar Everest flood warning system left to rust
events.description: The flood warning system at Imja glacial lake has not been maintained since 2016, fearful locals tell BBC.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Thousands at risk after multi-million dollar Everest flood warning system left to rust

events.title: Tanzania releases government-led inquiry into poll violence
events.description: In tonight's edition, a Tanzanian commission appointed by the President herself, releases its report into last year’s election violence. Also, Pope Leo wraps up his first major international tour with a Mass in Equatorial Guinea. And Congolese music star Fally Ipupa marks 20 years in the industry with new albums and concerts in Paris.

events.title: Long hours, stress and harassment are causing hundreds of thousands of early deaths, says UN labour agency
events.description: More than 840,000 people die each year from health conditions linked to risks such as long working hours, job insecurity, workplace harassment and bullying, according to a new report by the International Labour Organization (ILO).

events.title: Israel continues attacks on Lebanon despite extension of ceasefire
events.description: Israeli forces killed six Hezbollah fighters in an exchange of fire in the area of Bint Jbeil, southern Lebanon.

events.title: Beloved teacher killed as she slept, daughter stabbed in attack as motive remains unclear: DA

events.title: UK position on Falklands will not change, No 10 says after leaked Pentagon memo
events.description: Internal email proposes US should reassess support for UK claim to islands because of lack of support for Iran war UK politics live – latest updates Downing Street has been forced to insist that Britain will not yield sovereignty over the Falkland Islands, after a leaked Pentagon email proposed the US should reassess its support for the UK’s claim on the islands because of a lack of British support over Iran. The memo reflected ways in which the Trump administration could punish Britain for failing to follow the US lead in bombing Iran, and comes before a potentially fraught three-day state visit to the US by King Charles. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 UK position on Falklands will not change, No 10 says after leaked Pentagon memo

events.title: Iran's FM Araghchi arrives in Pakistan ahead of planned US ceasefire talks
events.description: Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi arrived in the Pakistani ​capital Islamabad on Friday to discuss proposals for restarting peace talks with the United States, amid conflicting signals on whether he would meet with US negotiators there. The White House said special envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner would leave for Pakistan on Saturday morning to continue talks with Iran.

events.description: Almost 4 years since a peace agreement signed between the Tigray People's Liberation Front and the Ethiopian government, hundreds of thousands of Tigrayans are still displaced and cannot return to their homes. Also, 518 people killed in the post-election violence that followed last octobeer presidential election in Tanzania that is the official figure issued by a government-appointed commission of inquiry. And, the 4th edition of the Marrakech African Literature Festival is underway, under the banner "imagining other possibilities".

events.title: Ceasefire extension offers diplomatic opening, but tensions persist in Strait of Hormuz
events.description: The United States’ decision to extend a fragile ceasefire with Iran has kept a narrow window open for diplomacy, but fresh security incidents in the Strait of Hormuz on Wednesday underscore the volatility of the situation and the risks to global shipping and regional stability.

events.title: Florida officials investigate planned ‘Sloth World’ attraction after 31 sloths die in warehouse
events.description: Languorous tree dwellers from Guyana and Peru died from ‘cold stun’ in warehouse with no power or running water Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email Wildlife officials in Florida said in a newly released report that dozens of sloths taken from South American rainforests for display at a controversial new tourist attraction in Orlando died in the care of their new owners. An incident report from the Florida fish and wildlife conservation commission (FWC) said that 31 of the mammals procured from Peru and Guyana by the owners of a forthcoming attraction called Sloth World perished in a storage warehouse more than a year ago, between December 2024 and February 2025. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Florida officials investigate planned ‘Sloth World’ attraction after 31 sloths die in warehouse

events.title: ‘Look, no hands’: China chases the driverless dream at Beijing car show
events.description: As domestic sales slow, manufacturers are investing in AI and seeking growth in technology and in overseas markets At the world’s biggest car fair, which opened in Beijing on Friday, there were hundreds of manufacturers, more than 1,000 vehicles, hundreds of thousands of enthusiasts – and hardly anyone behind a wheel. China’s car companies have cornered the domestic electric vehicle market, and are increasingly visible on the global stage . Now they are turning their attention to what they are betting is the future of mobility: autonomous driving. Continue reading...

events.title: Cuban ambassador says U.S. is to blame for island's crisis

events.title: US: Justice Department drops investigation into Fed Chair Powell

events.title: Poisoning suspected after 18 wolves found dead in Italian national park

events.title: California millionaire trampled to death by elephants while on African hunting expedition
events.description: California vineyard tycoon Ernie Dosio, 75, was killed by elephants during a hunting expedition in Gabon's Lope-Okanda rainforest, reports say.

events.title: Anthony Albanese accused of ‘caving to gas companies’ as Labor set to reject new export tax
events.description: David Pocock says prime minister – who is trying to shore up fuel supplies – is parroting industry talking points Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Labor is poised to reject a growing push for a new 25% tax on gas exports in next month’s budget, prompting David Pocock to accuse the government of “caving in” to the gas industry. It’s understood the government has elected not to pursue a new tax on gas exports in the budget , prompted in part by the global oil crisis and Anthony Albanese’s diplomatic efforts in shoring up fuel supply from Asian allies by pledging reliable access to liquefied natural gas. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Anthony Albanese accused of ‘caving to gas companies’ as Labor set to reject new export tax

events.title: Amid fuel crisis, a bold move to leave oil and gas behind

events.title: WHO says billions saw health gains in 2025 despite funding cuts
events.description: Despite significant funding cuts, the World Health Organization (WHO) was able to support significant national health gains for hundreds of millions of people in 2025, according to its annual Results Report released on Thursday.

events.title: They bought an abandoned 'ghost house' in the Japanese countryside

events.description: Palestinians take to the polls on Saturday, for the first time since the Gaza war, to vote in municipal elections. There are no Hamas candidates on electoral lists, which are mostly aligned with president Mahmud Abbas's secular-nationalist Fatah party or independents.

events.title: Indigenous speakers booed at Anzac Day services while Ben Roberts-Smith attends separate Gold Coast event
events.description: Roberts-Smith, who has denied five charges of war crime murder, says he was always going to attend: ‘I never thought about not coming’ Marcia Langton: The AFL bans disruptive racists. Surely police can do the same for morons who boo welcome to country Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Booing has marred Anzac Day commemorations in Sydney, Melbourne and Perth, while on the Gold Coast, the Victoria Cross recipient Ben Roberts-Smith attended the dawn service at Currumbin beach. One man was arrested at the Sydney dawn service at Martin Place, where there was a small but noisy interjection of booing during the Indigenous acknowledgment of country. Continue reading...

events.description: The United Nations is deeply alarmed by continued insecurity in the Darfur region of Sudan and the heavy toll it is having on civilians, Spokesperson Stéphane Dujarric said on Wednesday.

events.title: Trump officials consider sending 1,100 Afghans who aided US forces to Congo
events.description: Discussions reportedly come after Trump’s decision to stop initiative that allowed group to apply to resettle in the US The Trump administration is in discussions to potentially send up to 1,100 Afghans who helped US forces during the war in Afghanistan to the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC), a non-profit confirmed on Tuesday. The resettlement talks, first reported by the New York Times , come after Donald Trump’s decision to stop an initiative that allowed Afghans who assisted US war efforts to apply to resettle in the US. Continue reading...

Lai was to leave on Wednesday for the 40th anniversary of King Mswati III’s accession. Continue reading...

events.title: Soundtrack of the sea: divers use underwater speakers to help dying coral reefs
events.description: Divers are installing waterproof speakers in the ocean to help pull a coral reef near Jamaica back from the brink The northern coast of Jamaica once served as the backdrop for scenes in the James Bond thriller No Time to Die. But today, beneath those same turquoise waves, a real-life mission is unfolding: the race to pull a dying coral reef back from the brink. However, the tools a team of divers are carrying to the seafloor are not what you would expect to find in a marine biologist’s kit. They are installing waterproof speakers at the bottom of the ocean, and the man leading the team is not a scientist. Continue reading...

events.description: Discovered using AI, a naturally occurring molecule appears to act directly in the brain's appetite center. Scientists hope it could refine treatments for obesity, offering fewer side effects for some patients.

events.title: Nepal: Mount Everest ice block obstructs large group of spring climbers
events.description: A chunk of ice suspended above the Khumbu Icefall near Base Camp on Mount Everest means it's not safe either for climbers or the specialist "icefall doctors" who typically clear away smaller obstructions.

events.title: 'Information leakage to our adversaries': When prediction markets spiral out of control
events.description: A US Army soldier has been indicted on Thursday for using classified military intelligence to place winning bets on former Venezuelan leader Nicolas Maduro's capture. This is the latest and most explosive case in a growing pattern of insider trading on prediction markets such as Polymarket and Kalshi.
events.ticker_label: 💥 'Information leakage to our adversaries': When prediction markets spiral out of control

events.title: Retired US Army Colonel discusses naval strategy in Hormuz
events.description: Joe Buccino is the former communications director of US Central Command, CentCom, which directs and commands American military operations in the Middle East, Central and Sout Asia.

events.title: Crossing borders: The EU powers your mobility (part 1)
events.description: In this two-part show, Armen Georgian takes viewers on a journey across the European Union, exploring mobility and transport projects funded by the EU’s cohesion policy. In the 2021-2027 budget, the EU has been using its cohesion funds to invest €63.2 billion in transport schemes. From France to Austria, via Poland and Hungary, Armen takes a whistle-stop tour using seven different means of transport: train, plane, metro, tram, bicycle, scooter, and car.

events.title: Crossing borders: The EU powers your mobility (part 2)
events.description: In this second part of Europe Rendezvous, Armen Georgian is in Hungary and Austria on his whistle-stop journey across the EU, exploring mobility and transport projects funded by the EU's cohesion policy.

events.title: Pirate gangs prowl Bangladesh's Sundarbans forest
events.description: Those who have crossed their path fear them more than the tigers in Bangladesh's Sundarbans mangrove forest. Once thought largely subdued after mass surrenders a decade ago, the gangs have returned to the forest. Their resurgence – fuelled largely by poverty and debt – has again made the forest's labyrinth of rivers feared.

events.title: Why is Cuba a US target again? | Sky News Explains
events.description: It's no secret that Donald Trump's focus is turning to Cuba.

events.title: Australia and New Zealand gather in Turkey to commemorate WWI battle
events.description: Officials and visitors from Australia, New Zealand and Turkey gather in northwest Turkey to commemorate the 111th anniversary of the Battle of Gallipoli

events.title: Colombian leader visits Venezuela for key talks with acting President Delcy Rodríguez
events.description: Venezuela's acting president, Delcy Rodríguez, has welcomed Colombian President Gustavo Petro at the Miraflores presidential palace in Caracas

events.title: Kenyan appeal court overturns ruling that affirmed the right to abortion
events.description: A court of appeal in Kenya has overturned a ruling that affirmed the right to access abortion

events.title: After last year's fighting with Thailand, Cambodia readies new conscription law for men aged 18-25
events.description: Cambodia's Cabinet has approved a law requiring males aged 18 to 25 to serve two years in the military

events.title: Kosovo court sentences 3 ethnic Serbs to life, 30 years in prison over 2023 clash
events.description: A court in Kosovo has sentenced two ethnic Serbs to life in prison and another to 30 years over a 2023 clash with police that left four people dead

events.title: Bill to allow assisted dying in England and Wales is set to fall as parliamentary time runs out
events.description: A proposed bill to allow terminally ill adults in England and Wales to choose to end their lives is set to fall Friday as parliamentary time runs out

events.title: Japan's Haruki Murakami to publish first new novel in 3 years with 'The Tale of KAHO' in July
events.description: A new book by Haruki Murakami set to be released in early July will mark the first time a full-length novel by the Japanese author features a female main character

events.title: Prosecutors seek 30-year prison term for South Korea's Yoon
events.description: South Korean prosecutors are requesting a 30-year prison term for ousted President Yoon Suk Yeol

events.title: China to send giant pandas to Atlanta again
events.description: China is sending two giant pandas to Zoo Atlanta, the China Wildlife Conservation Association says

events.title: For every generation, vaccines work and they have saved over 150 million lives: WHO
events.description: Over the past 50 years, vaccines have saved more than 150 million lives, as ordinary people chose to protect themselves, their children and their communities from diseases like measles, diphtheria, pertussis, polio, and rotavirus.

events.title: Time to apply the brakes to runaway AI, says pioneer
events.description: If AI is “a very fast car with no steering wheel” then regulation must provide one, insists Nobel laureate and Artificial Intelligence pioneer Geoffrey Hinton, the visionary scientist widely known as the “godfather” of the self-learning tech.

events.title: Grinning suspect cuffed after alleged fiery street takeover, drag race caught on camera
events.description: The Queens DA announced the arrest of Asil Assaidi, who allegedly helped wreak havoc during a drag racing street takeover involving more than 100 vehicles.

events.title: Texas substitute teacher accused of improper relationship with student, alarming parents
events.description: Authorities arrested former substitute teacher Angela Palmares in Texas after she was allegedly involved in an improper relationship with a student.

events.title: Florida mayor says she 'did not resist' arrest over alleged probation violation from DUI case
events.description: Naples Mayor Teresa Heitmann was arrested for an alleged probation violation from a DUI case but says she voluntarily surrendered to authorities.

events.title: Fox News True Crime Newsletter: Zodiac Killer's victim, Gilgo Beach killer's ex-wife, Nancy Guthrie's DNA
events.description: Stay up to date with the Fox News True Crime Newsletter, which brings you the latest cases ripped from the headlines, from crime to courts, legal and scandal.

events.title: UC Berkeley slammed after anti-Israel group hosts failed suicide bomber as guest event speaker: 'cesspool'
events.description: UC Berkeley Law Students for Justice Palestine capped off Palestinian Political Prisoners Day with a video from a convicted attempted car bomber.
events.ticker_label: 💥 UC Berkeley slammed after anti-Israel group hosts failed suicide bomber as guest event speaker: 'cesspool'

events.title: Elizabeth Smart flexes new chapter, racks up bodybuilding honors in stunning transformation
events.description: Elizabeth Smart reveals her bodybuilding journey, winning first place in Utah and competing at the NPC Heart of Texas on her path to going pro.

events.title: US targets Chinese refinery in sweeping Iran oil crackdown, sanctions ‘shadow fleet’ tankers
events.description: The OFAC sanctioned a major Chinese oil refinery and dozens of Iran-linked shadow fleet vessels in an escalation of a maximum pressure campaign on Tehran.

events.title: US economic chokehold on Iran reaches peak leverage as collapse risks emerge
events.description: U.S. economic pressure on Iran has reached historic levels, but inconsistent enforcement limits full impact of sanctions, a former Treasury expert says.

events.title: Iran escalates Hormuz 'tit-for-tat,' seizes ship tied to billionaire close to Trump, Macron
events.description: Analysts say the IRGC seized two vessels in the Strait of Hormuz in a tit-for-tat move after a U.S. naval blockade and seizure of an Iranian ship.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Iran escalates Hormuz 'tit-for-tat,' seizes ship tied to billionaire close to Trump, Macron

events.title: Ex-Philippine president Duterte to face trial on crimes against humanity charges
events.description: The ICC confirmed crimes against humanity charges against former Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte for allegedly overseeing deadly drug crackdowns.

events.title: Trump envoy to Turkey doubles down after backlash, pushes ‘peace through strength’ policy
events.description: U.S. Ambassador to Turkey and Syria envoy Tom Barrack defends controversial remarks on Hezbollah, Turkey’s F-35 access and Middle East governance as pragmatic realism, not policy shifts.
